reasoning: While the fidelity score of 0.8649 is high, the specific evidence points to content and structural loss that affects document utility. The omission of contact information on the final page (URL and email) is described as exceeding major defect thresholds, and the conversion of ordered lists to unordered lists represents a loss of semantic hierarchy. Most importantly, the loss of instructional images/screenshots in an accessibility training document significantly diminishes its educational value.

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: In the final section 'Get in touch' (Page 34), two critical pieces of contact information are missing: the website URL ('Web: www.ablr360.com') and the email address ('Email: kim.casey@ablr360.com'). This represents an omission of 2 out of 6 main text elements in that section (33.3%), which exceeds the 25% threshold for a major defect.

Several numbered lists in the source PDF have been converted into unordered lists in the output. Specifically, the lists on pages 6, 7, 9, and 10 use numbers (1, 2, 3...) in the source, but the JSON defines them as 'unordered' and omits the numbers from the text content. This changes the structural meaning of the list from an ordered sequence to a simple set of items.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 6: '1. Use underline', '2. Can use...'; Page 7: '1. Enter field name', '2. Select...', '3. Add...', '4. Save...'; Page 9: '1. Add radio buttons', '2. Select...', '3. Enter...', '4. Enter...', '5. Enter...'; Page 10: '1. Use Preview...', '2. To clear...', '3. Select Clear...'
concerns.output_quote: Reading Order 24 (Page 6), 32 (Page 7), 41 (Page 9), 46 (Page 10): all lists marked as 'list_type': 'unordered' with numbers removed from text.

concerns.analysis: On page 34, the contact information includes a website and an email address that are clearly visible and formatted as links in the source PDF. These two elements (and their associated URLs) are entirely missing from the output text and structure, while the phone number is preserved. This constitutes a failure to preserve visible links.
concerns.output_quote: The text elements and links for the website and email are absent between 'Ablr' (index 165) and 'Call: 833-294-2257' (index 167).

concerns.analysis: A significant number of meaningful instructional images from the source PDF are missing in the rendered HTML. While some screenshots have been replaced by tables, many others—such as keyboard photos, software menus, and specific UI panels—are entirely absent. This includes critical instructional visuals like the Acrobat Tags panel (p.11), keyboard shortcut properties (p.15), keyboard photos for NVDA and VoiceOver (p.16, 18, 26, 27, 28), and the QR code on the final slide (p.34).
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source PDF contains instructional screenshots on pages 10, 11, 12, 15, 21, 25, 29, 30, and 32, as well as keyboard photos on pages 16, 18, 26, 27, and 28, and a QR code on page 34.
concerns.output_quote: The corresponding sections in the HTML output (e.g., 'Form Tags in Adobe Acrobat', 'Creating a Keyboard Shortcut', 'VoiceOver Resources', etc.) are missing these images.
concerns.location: Multiple sections throughout the document, specifically slides 10-12, 15, 16, 18, 21, 25-30, 32, and 34.

action_items: Verify the presence of contact links on page 34.
Restore the correct list types (ordered/numbered) on pages 6-10.
Assess whether the missing instructional screenshots need to be manually re-inserted or provided with alternative text.
